repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem:
- write_blob, write_string dereference args which may be NULL.
- `writefile(v:_null_blob, …)` fails.

Solution:
- Fix the annotation.
- Handle null blob.

---

- ~~There is not really a good reason to disallow Vimscript to pass String to writefile(), so just allow it.~~
- ~~fix: write_blob, write_string should not allow null args~~



cc @barrettruth

@@ -592,9 +594,9 @@ func Test_write_file_encoding()
   e Xcp866
   .w ++enc=cp1251 >> Xtest
   let expected =<< trim END

-    2 cp1251 text: ��� Vim version 6.2.  ��������� ���������: 1970 Jan 01
-    3 cp866 text: ��� Vim version 6.2.  ��������� ���������: 1970 Jan 01

comment_data.body: even though we dont need to check `script_is_lua` here, I kept the change to `nlua_call` from https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/39098

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1759,16 +1758,22 @@ void f_writefile(typval_T *argvars, typval_T *rettv, EvalFuncData fptr)
     return;
   }
 
+  // XXX: this is weird because of how `decode_string` works (called by `object_to_vim_take_luaref`):
+  // - if decode_string finds NUL in the Lua string, it assigns VAR_BLOB
+  // - else it assigns VAR_STRING
+  // TODO(justinmk):
+  // - figure out how decode_string can opportunistically skip that "NUL scan" when dispatching to
+  //   vimfns like f_writefile.

comment_regular_data.body: Didn't you say this shouldn't be allowed in a previous PR?

with zeertzjq on this - what was the selling point of this?

reading the previous prs *you* convinced me to intelligently make only the neovim-layer api calls diverge from vim so vim-layer is the same... xd

> makes the logic for handling Lua strings weird

if you are convinced, can you give a more concrete example of why this would be a problem?

Yes I did [advise against this](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/31930#discussion_r1913181230).  And I did callout my misunderstanding in this PR description...

> (if someone suggested this already then I missed it or misunderstood, sorry :)



But in my defense, none of the previous PRs explained what is actually going on. Having looked at the code more closely now, I have (1) explained it and (2) changed my mind. 

I didn't realize that Lua strings may be either VAR_STRING or VAR_BLOB *depending on their contents*. That's a low-level issue with `decode_string`. But that makes the logic here weird, and at minimum requires more explanation in a code comment.
dropped the `allow writefile({string}) from Vimscript` change, this only fixes the annotations now

> Anyway I don't think changing the annotations makes sense here.

if `data=NULL` then `write_string` does `strlen(NULL)`

title: fix(eval): writestring() handling of null
